info.name: text-delete
info.text: An idea is practically invisible when it is first formed

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A brand new idea is a seed, almost worthless on its own, but with great potential.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: One needs to be patient when nurturing an idea, as it will be a slow development process

info.name: text-delete
info.text: You can pass ideas along easily, perhaps more easily than finished products.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: As your ideas develop, you need to be careful not to give them too little, or even too much, attention

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A good idea, with care and some luck, will bear real fruit

info.name: text-delete
info.text: An idea needs a well made bed to be its foundation before it can go anywhere.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: An idea might live forever, but it can always keep growing

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Even the best idea can wither and die if it is neglected.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Like seeds in a field, ideas can be stolen

info.name: text-insert
info.text: Ideas grow and change, but they always have a place in our minds.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas grow and change, but they always have a place in our minds.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas might seem valuable to the person who owns them, but only the grown flower appeals to outsiders

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Scavengers might feed off of your ideas before they can bloom

info.name: text-delete
info.text: An idea may be vulnerable and weak at first, but it could grow to be solid and hardy

info.name: text-delete
info.text: As in the bottom of the food chain, at the basic level, all activity ultimately depends upon ideas to thrive.
